name: Observability
summary: OpenTelemetry tracing instrumentation for HTTP requests and export to Datadog.

requirements.title: Automatic request trace spans
requirements.statement: The system SHALL automatically create an OpenTelemetry trace span for every incoming HTTP request handled by the Hono service.

acceptance.given: the Hono service is running with OpenTelemetry enabled
acceptance.outcome: a root span is created for that request
the span is ended and exported

requirements.title: Export trace data to Datadog
requirements.statement: The system SHALL export generated trace data to Datadog.

acceptance.given: the service is configured with a valid Datadog endpoint or Datadog Agent
acceptance.outcome: the spans are delivered to Datadog
the service continues to handle requests without crashing

requirements.title: HTTP semantic attributes on request spans
requirements.statement: The system SHALL include standard HTTP semantic attributes on each request span, such as HTTP method, route, and status code.

acceptance.outcome: the span contains the attributes `http.method`, `http.route`, and `http.status_code`

requirements.title: Trace context propagation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL propagate OpenTelemetry trace context across inbound and outbound HTTP calls.

acceptance.given: an inbound request contains a valid `traceparent` header
acceptance.when: the Hono service processes the request
acceptance.outcome: the existing trace context is continued rather than a new trace being started

acceptance.given: the service makes an outbound HTTP call

requirements.title: Environment-variable configuration for OpenTelemetry and Datadog
requirements.statement: The system SHALL allow OpenTelemetry and Datadog export settings to be configured via environment variables.

acceptance.given: the service is starting and environment variables for OpenTelemetry and Datadog export are set
acceptance.when: the service initialises its telemetry stack
acceptance.outcome: the service applies those values without requiring code changes

acceptance.given: the service is starting and no deployment-specific environment variables are set
acceptance.when: the service initialises its telemetry stack
acceptance.outcome: the service starts using sensible defaults

requirements.title: Negligible request latency from instrumentation
requirements.statement: The system SHALL add negligible latency to request processing under normal load.

acceptance.given: the service is running under normal load with OpenTelemetry enabled
acceptance.outcome: the additional latency per request remains negligible compared to the baseline without instrumentation

requirements.title: No sensitive data in telemetry
requirements.statement: The system SHALL not include sensitive data such as passwords, tokens, or PII in span attributes or logs.

acceptance.given: a request contains an `Authorization` header, password field, or token
acceptance.when: the corresponding span is created and exported
acceptance.outcome: the span attributes and logs do not contain the sensitive value

acceptance.given: a request contains PII in its path, query, or body
acceptance.when: the corresponding span is created and exported
acceptance.outcome: the span attributes and logs do not contain the PII

rules.rule: Instrumentation must integrate with Hono's request lifecycle and middleware model.
rules.rationale: The service is built on the Hono framework; OpenTelemetry instrumentation must participate naturally in Hono middleware and request handling.

rules.rule: Trace export must target Datadog through a Datadog-compatible exporter or the Datadog Agent's OTLP intake.
rules.rationale: Datadog is the required telemetry backend, so the exporter or intake path must be compatible with Datadog.

rules.rule: Trace context propagation must follow the W3C Trace Context standard.
rules.rationale: Standardised propagation ensures interoperability with other OpenTelemetry-instrumented callers and downstream services.
